I’m unclear about a conflict in your request. You asked for papers on blockchain topics like "consensus protocols, proof-of-work, proof-of-stake," but then asked to exclude any papers about "distributed systems (consensus algorithms, fault tolerance...)." Consensus, PoW and PoS papers are usually classified under distributed systems, so strict exclusion would rule out most consensus literature.
Do you mean:

- Exclude papers focused on cryptographic primitives only (e.g., new ZK proofs, new hash or signature schemes) and on core distributed-systems theory (e.g., Paxos, PBFT proofs), but allow applied/economic/empirical or security papers about PoW/PoS and smart contracts? OR
- Exclude all distributed systems work entirely (in which case I should omit consensus/PoW/PoS papers and only list application/economic/legal/smart-contract-analysis papers)?
Please confirm which interpretation you want, and whether papers about smart-contract security and formal verification are allowed.
